preamble: The transformation of the voiced labiodental fricative /v/ into the palatal glide /j/ is an uncommon sound change driven by palatalization and lenition. The articulation moves from the lips and teeth to the hard palate, and the friction is lost, resulting in a glide. This is most likely before /i/ or /j/.

languageExamples.description: Portuguese historical developments or English dialects.

sources: Ladefoged, P., & Maddieson, I. (1996). The Sounds of the World's Languages.
Wells, J. C. (1982). Accents of English.
